Purpose: Discarded or parked ideas moved out of the active backlog so backlog.json stays focused on the near-term experiment queue.

Items.Title: Persist reusable pixel snapshots per captured frame
Items.Hypothesis: Converting each frame bitmap into a reusable snapshot once should cut repeated PixelBuffer.FromBitmap cost across zone detection and overlap search.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. The prototype only moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 4577/1210/1112 ms to 4428/1173/1162 ms (suite total 6899 -> 6763, about 2.0% faster overall) and slightly regressed synthetic-up, which was not enough to justify the extra detector/cache seam.

Items.Title: Freeze zone estimation after stable consensus and only invalidate on disagreement
Items.Hypothesis: Avoiding full-history zone recomputation after convergence should eliminate wasted rescans on long monotonic captures.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after the pair-analysis cache landed. Remaining zone-estimation work is now a cheap scan over cached pair results, so freezing consensus would add invalidation complexity for little expected payoff.

Items.Title: Add a coarse row or column fingerprint pass before pixel refinement
Items.Hypothesis: Cheap 1D fingerprints can reject most impossible overlaps before expensive per-pixel comparisons.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. A behavior-safe prototype that used coarse fingerprints only as a prefilter plus full-fidelity fallback over skipped larger candidates moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 1086/285/299 ms to 1130/294/291 ms (suite total 1670 -> 1715), so it was slightly slower than the accepted SIMD baseline.

Items.Title: Extract band snapshots directly from source bitmaps without intermediate band clones
Items.Hypothesis: Avoiding temporary band bitmap clones for pair analysis and no-match paths should reduce allocation and GDI overhead while preserving the current overlap logic.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. A direct bitmap-region snapshot prototype moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 1086/285/299 ms to 1096/350/339 ms (suite total 1670 -> 1785), so it was clearly slower than the accepted SIMD baseline.

Items.Title: Reuse the latest analyzed pair snapshot during the immediate incremental append
Items.Hypothesis: The newest pair analysis already materializes the current band snapshot, so reusing that snapshot during the immediate append step should remove one more temporary extraction/conversion without paying the memory cost of a full history snapshot cache.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. The prototype moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 933/220/222 ms to 931/206/237 ms (suite total 1375 -> 1374), which is effectively noise and not enough to justify extra state in the session.

Items.Title: Share luma and 1D signal precompute across forward and reverse bidirectional matching
Items.Hypothesis: Bidirectional matching currently runs the same luma projection and profile-building work twice for the same frame pair, so sharing that preprocessing across forward and reverse overlap evaluation should shave more time off the hot path.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. Sharing the precompute only moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 250/61/74 ms to 244/65/71 ms (suite total 385 -> 380, about 1.3% faster overall) while slightly regressing synthetic-down, which was not enough to justify extra matcher surface area.

Items.Title: Drop alpha from the exact overlap scoring path
Items.Hypothesis: Most captured bands carry constant alpha, so ignoring that channel in exact scoring could cut hot-path work without materially changing overlap decisions.
Items.Outcome: Discarded as superseded after the retained luma-only overlap scorer landed. The current exact path already projects to grayscale and therefore drops alpha along with most per-channel work, so a separate alpha-only experiment is no longer worth running.

Items.Title: Short-circuit row-level SAD once the remaining threshold budget is exhausted
Items.Hypothesis: Pushing threshold-aware early exit down into the row-level SIMD/scalar SAD kernel should let exact overlap candidates fail within a row instead of only between rows.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. The row-budgeted SAD prototype moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 186/52/67 ms to 200/58/60 ms (suite total 305 -> 318), so the extra checkpointing overhead outweighed the finer-grained short-circuiting.

Items.Title: Parallelize independent pair analysis during history rescans
Items.Hypothesis: Adjacent-pair zone and overlap checks are independent enough to exploit multiple cores when rescans are still necessary.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after the pair-analysis cache landed. History rescans are now lightweight enough that parallel coordination overhead is unlikely to beat the remaining single-threaded work.

Items.Title: Use rolling hashes or integral summaries for overlap candidates
Items.Hypothesis: Rolling summaries could turn overlap search from repeated full-slice comparisons into cheaper incremental updates.
Items.Outcome: Parked after the coarse-fingerprint family failed to beat the SIMD baseline. Not worth revisiting unless a future projection-based pass proves candidate pruning can win overall.

Items.Title: Prototype a native OpenCV or phase-correlation matcher
Items.Hypothesis: A vision-library backend might deliver a much better accuracy-speed frontier than the current pure C# brute-force matcher.
Items.Outcome: Parked. Still interesting, but too invasive while there are cheaper in-process ideas left to try.

Items.Title: Evaluate GPU overlap scoring for large scroll bands
Items.Hypothesis: Large overlap scans are embarrassingly parallel and could move well to ComputeSharp, DirectCompute, or CUDA-like kernels.
Items.Outcome: Parked. Too invasive and operationally heavy for the current branch-local optimization loop.

Items.Title: Swap System.Drawing hot paths for a faster imaging stack
Items.Hypothesis: SkiaSharp, ImageSharp, or a custom native buffer layer could reduce clone, crop, and compose overhead across the whole pipeline.
Items.Outcome: Parked. Could still matter eventually, but not before exhausting tighter matcher/session optimizations.

Items.Title: Move heavy stitching to a separate optimized worker process
Items.Hypothesis: A dedicated worker in another runtime or deployment model could unlock aggressive optimization without entangling the app process.
Items.Outcome: Parked. Architecture-heavy and out of proportion for the current experiment loop.
